title: Review: implement all backends + CI
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Three real bugs (Vault empty-secret, json_skip leniency, local-signing dispatch gap) plus two security-surface items (path traversal documentation, no cargo audit) that don't affect correct usage today but own the CVE or the support burden if users hit them. Nothing in the correctness findings is remotely exploitable given typical usage, but the file path traversal gap is the most defensible public security issue.
Confidence: 25% chance of a missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) backend crates for Azure KV, GCP SM, Bitwarden, and Doppler have no live-service tests and I reviewed the HTTP layer logic only statically; (2) the PKCS11 backend has complex session/slot lifetime management I did not fully trace; (3) the CachingStore eviction logic under concurrent write pressure. Would raise confidence if: integration tests existed for Azure KV and GCP SM, or if a fuzzer was run against json_extract_string_field with malformed JSON inputs.
